A: insert cell above

B: insert cell below

X: cut selected cells

C: copy selected cells

Shift-V: paste cells above

V: paste cells below

Z: undo cell deletion

D,D: delete selected cells

Shift-M: merge selected cells, or current cell with cell below if only one cell selected

Ctrl-S: Save and Checkpoint

S: Save and Checkpoint

L: toggle line numbers

O: toggle output of selected cells

Shift-O: toggle output scrolling of selected cells

H: show keyboard shortcuts

I,I: interrupt kernel

0,0: restart the kernel (with dialog)

Esc: close the pager

Q: close the pager

Shift-L: toggles line numbers in all cells, and persist the setting

Shift-Space: scroll notebook up

Space: scroll notebook down

Shortcuts in Edit Mode (press Enter to enable)

Tab: code completion or indent

Shift-Tab: tooltip

Ctrl-]: indent

Ctrl-[: dedent

Ctrl-A: select all

Ctrl-Z: undo

Ctrl-Shift-Z: redo

Ctrl-Y: redo

Ctrl-Home: go to cell start

Ctrl-Up: go to cell start

Ctrl-End: go to cell end

Ctrl-Down: go to cell end

Ctrl-Left: go one word left

Ctrl-Right: go one word right

Ctrl-Backspace: delete word before

Ctrl-Delete: delete word after

Ctrl-M: command mode

Ctrl-Shift-F: open the command palette

Ctrl-Shift-P: open the command palette

Esc: command mode

Shift-Enter: run cell, select below

Ctrl-Enter: run selected cells

Alt-Enter: run cell, insert below

Ctrl-Shift-Minus: split cell

Ctrl-S: Save and Checkpoint

Down: move cursor down

Up: move cursor up

Transform Jupyter Notebook to Other Format

.ipynb is a JSON document and can be transformed to other format easily by using nbconvert.

For example, you can type the code below in terminal to transform it to HTML.

jupyter nbconvert --to html notebook.ipynb

Of course, it is also available to slides.

jupyter nbconvert notebook.ipynb --to slides

For showing the slides at once, the function is 

jupyter nbconvert notebook.ipynb --to slides --post serve

entry.title: LeetCode Problems & Notes--Part I Array
entry.content:
This blog is the note for the problems and possible solutions of LeetCode. In fact LeetCode has provided numerous solutions for each question in the discussion part, but I still want to record the experiences and notes about each questions. I will seperate the questions by their tags, and this page is about array problems. what’s more, I will update this series of blog continuelly until I have finished all of the questions in LeetCode. I hope I can achieve this goal in six month, and wait for your reply in the discussion block below.

P.S. Since I am learning Java and Python3 nowadays, this time I will only update the solution by these two laguages, and add other languages later.

Easy-Question 561-Array Partition I

Question

Given an array of 2n integers, your task is to group these integers into n pairs of integer, say (a1, b1), (a2, b2), …, (an, bn) which makes sum of min(ai, bi) for all i from 1 to n as large as possible.

Examples

Input: [1,4,3,2]

Output: 4
Explanation: n is 2, and the maximum sum of pairs is 4 = min(1, 2) + min(3, 4).

Note

n is a positive integer, which is in the range of [1, 10000].

All the integers in the array will be in the range of [-10000, 10000].

Alogrithm

In order to understand this approach, let us look at the problem from a different perspective. We need to form the pairings of the array’s elements such that the overall sum of the minimum out of such pairings is maximum. Thus, we can look at the operation of choosing the minimum out of the pairing, say (a, b)(a,b) as incurring a loss of a - ba−b(if a> ba>b), in the maximum sum possible.

The total sum will now be maximum if the overall loss incurred from such pairings is minimized. This minimization of loss in every pairing is possible only if the numbers chosen for the pairings lie closer to each other than to the other elements of the array.

Taking this into consideration, we can sort the elements of the given array and form the pairings of the elements directly in the sorted order. This will lead to the pairings of elements with minimum difference between them leading to the maximization of the required sum.

Solution

Python3

class Solution:
def arrayPairSum(self, nums):
    """
       :type nums: List[int]
       :rtype: int
       """
       nums.sort()
       return sum(nums[::2])

notes

  1. sort() method on a list sorts that list into ascending order, e.g. list.sort().
  2. nums[A:B:C] means from the Ath number to Bth number in the list, choose one number ever C numbers.Thus, sum(nums[::2]) means add the first, third, fifth…… number of the list.

Java

class Solution {
    public int arrayPairSum(int[] nums) {
        Arrays.sort(nums);
        int sum = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < nums.length; i += 2){
             sum += nums[i];
        }
        return sum;
    }
}

notes

  1. Array.sort() method is a java.util.Arrays class method.

Easy-Question 1-Two Sum

Question

Given an array of integers, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to a specific target.

You may assume that each input would have exactly one solution, and you may not use the same element twice.

Examples

Given nums = [2, 7, 11, 15], target = 9,

Because nums[0] + nums[1] = 2 + 7 = 9,
return [0, 1].

Alogrithm

Solution

Python3

class Solution:
def twoSum(self, nums, target):        
    d = {}
    for index, item in enumerate(nums):
        minus = target - item
        if minus in d:
            return [d[minus], index]
        else:
            d[item] = index

notes

  1. enumerate is a function that return the indexs and items of a list.
  2. d={} is a dictionary to store the index and item of the original list, and it is also very convenient to search the value by index.

Java

class Solution {
    public int[] twoSum(int[] nums, int target) {
        Map<Integer, Integer> map = new HashMap<>();
        for (int i = 0; i< nums.length; i++){
            int minus = target - nums[i];
            if (map.containsKey(minus)){
                return new int[]{map.get(minus),i};
            }
            map.put(nums[i],i);
        }
        throw new IllegalArgumentException("No two sum solution");
    }
}

notes

  1. HashMap() likes the dictionary in Python, which is used to store the key and value.
  2. map.containsKey() is used to search the value by key.
  3. map.get() is used to get the value by key.
  4. map.put() is used to add a new element in the HashMap.
  5. throw new IllegalArgumentException() is used to show the error for the wrong answer.

entry.title: Algebraic Equation App Development
entry.content:
Hi everyone！

Welcome to my blog and I’d like to share my first project about Android APP development in this post. As a beginner in APP development, my first project is about an algebraic equation quiz. Users will answer ten questions about linear and quadratic equation, and the APP should judge the result with the correct answer to analysize their performance in this test. This blog is about some basic information of this APP.

1 Basic Information

I use Android Studio 2.3.3 to develop this APP and compile this in Nexus_S with API Level 23:5554.

This APP contains 3 pages: The start page, the quiz page(the first 5 questions are about linear equation and the next 5 questions are about quadratic equations) and the result page. Now I will introduce the functions in the following steps.

2 Start Page

The start page includes the title with three buttons. The START button links the quiz page, while the ABOUT button combines the URL of this webpage and will skip to this blog once click it. The DEMO button will skip to Youtube, where I’ve upload a demo for this App as a guidance.

3 Quiz Page

3.1 Linear Equation

The first 5 questions are about linear equation Ax+B=0 where A and B are two
random integers in the range [–99, 99]. The user will be asked to provide a
value of x in 2 decimal places.

Once the answer is verified as correct result, the UI will show Right with the total correct questions in green. What’s more, the using time is defined as the duration from the page appearance to the ENTER button clicking.

For linear equation, click ENTER with nothing input will be regard as wrong. Empty input and incorrect input will return the Error with the correct answer in red.

3.2 Quadratic Equation

Question 6 to 10 are about quadratic equation Ax^2 + Bx + C = 0 where A, B and
C are three random integers in the range [–99, 99]. Of course, the APP has verified that all the quadratic equations have real roots.

As linear equations, only two answers are verified as correct results will the UI shows the Right in green.

Empty input and incorrect input will be regard as wrong and return the Error information in red.

The differnce between the quadratic equation with the linear equation is the incomplete input. Input one correct answer and empty another will be regard as incomplete situation, while input one correct answer and one incorrect answer will be regard as error.

The figure above shows the incomplete situation.

While correct answer combines with the incorrect input will be regard as wrong answer.

4 Result Page

This is the last page in the APP, in this page the user will get the feedback about his or her performance in this test. Different situations are displayed in different color to highlight. What’s more, by click the source code below, you can get the source code of this App in GitHub.

P.S. I’ve upload the source code in my GitHub account, and you can download it in https://github.com/huuuuusy/Algebraic-Equation-App.

entry.title: Notes about Machine Learning in Coursera--week 1
entry.content:
Hi everyone！

As I’ve said in the homepage, I am interested in deep learning and want to share my learning notes in this website. As a novice in this area, I choose several ways to expand the scope of knowledge, and the very first is learning Machine Learning course by Andrew Ng in Coursera. In fact, I do recommond you to try this course since it will provide you a clear logic about machine learning with enough mathematical derivations.

However, you should finish assignments by using Matlab rather than Python, and it may not very convenient. If you want to learn deep learning in coursera by using Python, you can choose another series of courses provided by deeplearning.ai, and the lecturer is Andrew Ng as well. As choosing this course at the same time, I will also share my notes and codes in the next blogs.

In my perspective, the course has provided enough references for students to consult, so I just paste my notes made by iPad Pro with Apple pencil, and this is my personal comprehension about this course, for reference only. Any questions, please leave a message by using DISQUS in the bottom of this page.

Finally，thanks a lot for forgiving my poor English skills and calligraphy:)

P.S. This blog is the notes for the first week in Machine Learning course, and I will provide the codes(if have) and notes for the same week together in a blog.

entry.title: How to build a simple website--part 1
entry.content:
Hi everyone！

Thanks a lot for reading my blog, and this article is the first post of the seires–How to build a simple website. In this page, I’ll introduce the preparatory work for building a website, and what you need is just doing it step by step with enough patience. By the way, I hope you can forgive my poor English skill:)

Preface

There are two important steps for building a personal website.

The first is buying a special domain name such as yourname.com. You can get your domain in different Internet service providers, like GoDaddy, Alibaba Cloud and Tencent Cloud. I will use GoDaddy as an example to introduce detailed procedures since my domain is bought in this website. (Based on the cost consideration, choosing other providers may be a better choice and you can get detailed gudiance by some related blogs.)

For second step, you need to deploy your personal website in a space. Choosing a cloud host seems good, but it is difficult for novice to practice. In fact, Github provides a space(300M) for users to deploy their website, and the only thing that you need is just a Github account. Readers who do not need personal domain can start from step two, since Github will provide you a domain like https://github.com/your_name/your_project_name.

Let’s start now!

I. Buy a domain

It is very convenient to buy a domain in GoDaddy, and here are specific steps:

1. Search a domain that you want

P.S. You can choose Singapore as location in the upper left of this page and browse it in simplified Chinese version.

2. Add to shop cart

3. Pay for it

P.S. Dodaddy will show you a page to make sure if you need the extra service before you pay for the domain. It’s better to choose No.Thanks even if the service is not expensive since what we need is just a domain.

Now you can choose the term that you need(1/2/3/5/10 years) and pay for it. 3 years may be a good choice because you can renew this domain before the deadline if you want to keep using.

P.S. You can search GoDaddy promo code and get a discount by using the newest prommo code.

Before paying the bill, you need to login an account. Some necessary information is needed, and for successful payment, you’d better to fill it with the detailed personal information. For Chinese users, you can find solutions in Dute.me if you have any question for paying with Alipay.

4. Check your domain

After you paying for your domain, reLogin and check it in your account.

What’s more, you will receive an Email to verify your account, and remember to check it.

II Download some software

P.S. Images in this part are from beiyuu‘s blog.

1. Install Node.js and Git

Before deploying the website, we need to download two softwares.

Node.js

Git

I will skip the details since it is easy to install these softwares if you download the correct version.

2. Login Github

Access github and create your account.

P.S. Pay attention to your username and Email address, since Github always uses email to send important notification.

3. Configurate Github

Now we need some processes to connect the local project with remote Github.

Check SSH keys

For Windows, you can find Git Bash in the start menu and open it as a terminal, then type the instruction.

$ cd ~/.ssh

If you are new to git, it will shows No such file or directory, and you can skip next step.

Remove old SSH keys (Optional)

If there is an old key in PC, you need to back-up and remove it.

$ ls
$ mkdir key_backup
$ cp id_rsa* key_backup
$ rm id_rsa*

Generate the new key

$ ssh-keygen -t rsa -C "youremail@youremail.com" 
Generating public/private rsa key pair.
Enter file in which to save the key (/Users/your_user_directory/.ssh/id_rsa):<ENTER>

P.S. You need to type your email address in the first line, and just press ENTER to choose the default path.

Then you need to type a passphrase:

Enter passphrase (empty for no passphrase):<just enter a series of numbers that you like>
Enter same passphrase again:<enter again>

You will see an interface like this, which means you are successful to set the ssh key.

Add SSH key to Github

Open id_rsa.pub and copy the text correctly.

P.S. The reference path is C:\Users\usersname.ssh\id_rsa.pub. You need to show hidden files if you cannot see this file in the folder.

Login Github and click Account Settings–>SSH and GPG keys–>New SSH Key, then paste your SSH in the textbox and click Add key.

Test

Now we can type this instruction in Git to check weather it is success.

$ ssh -T git@GitHub.com

P.S. Do not change the email address in this instruction.

Then you can get a feedback like this:

The authenticity of host 'GitHub.com (207.97.227.239)' can't be established.
RSA key fingerprint is 16:27:ac:a5:76:28:2d:36:63:1b:56:4d:eb:df:a6:48.
Are you sure you want to continue connecting (yes/no)?

Now type yes and you will see:

Hi huuuuusy! You've successfully authenticated, but GitHub does not provide shell access.

Set user’s information

Some personal information is needed to replenish in Git. Since user name and email will be used to record your submit and authorize management, it is better to type your true name in this instruction.

$ git config --global user.name "your_name"
$ git config --global user.email "your_email@youremail.com"

Now our PC have connected with Github successfully.

P.S. For any questions in setting SSH keys, you can consult these two pages:

GitHub Help - Generating SSH Keys

GitHub Help - Error Permission denied (publickey)

III Build a static blog frame

Other articles may have different order to operate, and I will choose a convenient one to introduce. What’s more, huuuuusy is my user name in Github, you should change it to your name in the following steps.

1. Introduction of Hexo

As the offical website advertises, Hexo is a fast, simple&powerful blog framework and you only need few commands to deploy your site to Github Pages. Plentiful themes are available in it, which are convenient to be changed or modified for personal demands.

2. Install Hexo

You can consult the guide in offical website, and install Hexo with npm in Git:

$ npm install -g hexo-cli

Now, consult guide and operate step by step to setup Hexo. First, create a folder in PC, and rename it as huuuuusy.github.io. Then, open Git Bash in this folder and tpye:

$ npm install

Once initialised, your folder will look like:

.
├── _config.yml
├── package.json
├── scaffolds
├── source
|   ├── _drafts
|   └── _posts
└── themes

You can design personal interface by change parameters in these files, and I will introduce this part in next blog, now just ignore these details. If you want to get more information in this part, please browse the offical website.

3. Install a theme(optional)

Hexo provides plentiful themes for users, and you can choose what you like in Hexo theme. For novice, Next may be a good choice since it is the most popular theme in Hexo with numerous guides and you can configure your personal interface by consult these articles.

The defaul theme is landscape, you can choose changing it to other themes by Googling different guides now or changing it after you have finished all necessary procedures. In next blog, I will introduce the specific process about design personal theme, so just skip this step now:)

4. Deploy Hexo

We can test the original interface of Hexo by this commond:

$ hexo g
$ hexo s

Now open the Explorer and type http://localhost:4000 in the address bar, you will see an interface like the theme that you choose in step 3.

By the way, after you changing some parameters to optimize the interface, you can use above commond to preview the effect.

IV Deploy the blog frame to GitHub Pages

Now open GitHub and create a new repository which name is huuuuusy.github.io since I’ve build a folder with the same name in part III. (Of course, you should change it to the name that you’ve set in the local folder.)

Open Git Bash and tpye commands as follow:

$ git add . 
$ git commit -m "first post" 
$ git remote add origin https://github.com/username/projectName.git 
# We need this command to push the local change to GitHub Page. In fact, you should change the URL as yours, like https://github.com/huuuuusy/huuuuusy.github.io.git, and you may need to type user name and password after that step
$ git push origin gh-pages

After that, we need to add some necessary information in -config.yml to finish the deployment.

Find the keyword “deploy” in this file and change it as follow:

deploy:
  type: git
  repository: https://github.com/huuuuusy/huuuuusy.github.io.git
  branch: master

Then open Git and type the following commands to complete deployment:

$ hexo g
$ hexo d

Now type the huuuuusy.github.io in the browser(need change it to yours), you’ll see a webpage like you’ve seen in http://localhost:400.

V Binding GitHub Page with your domain

1. Set the DNS server

I recommond DNSPod as the DNS server since it is convenient for you to organize the domain and you can experience other functions like cloud server in this website.

Login an account and add the domain that you’ve bought in GoDaddy, then supplement the following information:

P.S. The IP value of top two records are provided by GitHub Pages, and if you cannot find your website, you need to make sure if GitHub changes its IP address.

The last value is www, and you should change it to your repository in GitHub.

2. Set GoDaddy

Now come back to GoDaddy and change the default name servers to DNSPod.

Click manage DNS and change the default value to f1g1ns1.dnspod.net and f1g1ns2.dnspod.net.

3. Set GitHub repository

Open GitHub and create a new file names CNAME in huuuuusy.github.io, then recodes your domain in it.

Ok, wait for a few minutes to resolve the domain, then you can access your website by huuuuusy.github.io or hushiyu1995.com.

Epilogue

In this article, I introduce some basic procedures to complete a website with a special domain. Next blogs in this series will include some tips about optimizing the interface and writing new blogs with MarkDown.

Thanks a lot for reading my first post!

entry.title: Hello World
entry.content:
Hi everyone！Welcome to My page! This is my very first post. Check ABOUT.ME for more info.

As a begineer in Computer Science, I’d like to record my learning process in some interesting areas like Deep Learning, Multimedia and Web Design, and that is why I spend 5 days to build this primary personal website.

However, I have to say that some bugs like the unsuitable display on mobile terminal will bother the browsing experience, and I will try to solve it in the future.

What’s more, several useful functions like online comment and page sharing are available now, hope these can help you experience my page better.

For this website, I’d like to say thanks to GoDaddy, which provides me a special domain (although it is expensive); and DNSPod, which provides DNS service for free. Github is really an useful tool and helps me to deploy my website. Hexo ,a simple blog framework, includes lots of creative ideas and themes. And thanks to Viosey for your amazing blog project in github.

Thanks a lot for reading my first post, and I will compose my blog from now on.

P.S. Please forgive my terrible English writing skill:)
